recentTweets.text:
What is the U.S. Postal Service’s new plan that would slow down mail delivery?
 
Let’s find out. https://t.co/Yn23UIC4XB

I’ve introduced a new bipartisan bill that will help students graduate faster and at a lower cost.

The Credit for Prior Learning Act will make it possible for more Americans to afford their education by allowing students to earn college credit for learning they received outside

If there's a birthday, you've got to have some cake! 🎂🇺🇸  The 250th birthday cake was unveiled with the Smithsonian & the Library of Congress. And Minnesota made its mark: I added our state bird the loon & a pair of hockey sticks with cake artist Grace Pak. https://t.co/gw0jrIBkJx

Remembering my dad, a veteran himself, on our nation’s 250th today!

To all our veterans, service members, and military families – thank you. 🇺🇲 https://t.co/SV1vT52KSX

From parades to fireworks, today we celebrate 250 years of the American spirit and our founding ideals – the right to life, liberty, and the pursuit of happiness.

Happy Independence Day, America! 🇺🇲
Nothing like northwestern and northern Minnesota in the summer 🇺🇸 … with our friends in Canada 🇨🇦 right across the river! https://t.co/N5uhCC0A7m

Stop 8 of my Northwest & Northern Minnesota tour: Koochiching County! 
 
Rainy Lake Medical Center in International Falls, with a staff of over 200, plays a critical role in ensuring this region has access to 24/7 emergency care, primary care, and clinic services. https://t.co/V0rvYxuo2W

Stop 7 of my rural tour: Lake of the Woods County!

At New Moon Coffeehouse in Baudette, I joined local leaders & volunteers ahead of their Walleye Fish Fry and July 4th celebrations — with so many visitors coming in to mark our nation’s 250th birthday & enjoy the outdoors! 🏕️🎇 https://t.co/7t2W4oGpUF

Stop 6 on my rural tour: Roseau County!
 
Met with the Mayor & leaders of Lil’ Chompers Childcare in Greenbush to discuss their new facility, which is being built with federal funding I helped secure. The new facility will provide 40 kids with care — including needed infant care. https://t.co/wrrgbmHwFJ

Stop 5 of my rural tour: Kittson County!
 
Enjoyed great coffee with local small business owners, entrepreneurs, & leaders at Bully Brew Coffee in Hallock. 
 
To help our businesses thrive, we need to lower costs and make it easier to grow and create jobs. https://t.co/6YXprSAVY1

Stop 4 of my rural tour: Warren!

I met with local leaders about how Warren stepped up to address child care needs with the Little Sprouts Learning Center. When parents can’t find care, they can’t go to work, & local economies suffer. We must support local solutions like this. https://t.co/NKrsCOxjc2
Great way to kick off our country’s 250th birthday: a visit to one of Minnesota's many innovators – Arctic Cat — here in Pennington County! https://t.co/3aJN7LTAia

Stop 3 of my rural tour: Thief River Falls — home to Arctic Cat, where snowmobiles and ATVs are manufactured right here in northwest Minnesota.

Last year they returned to independent ownership, they’re thriving, and they’re one of this region’s largest employers.

Here with https://t.co/OTWQijujCY

Now THIS is a Minnesota view! 🌳🌊

From kayaking on the scenic Red Lake River to their campground, Voyageur's View Camping, Tubing, & Kayaking is a beautiful spot here in Red Lake Falls. @VoyageursView 

Places like this help showcase all that Northwest MN has to offer. https://t.co/XgfbPWlzeb

Stop 2 on my Northwest & Northern Minnesota tour: Red Lake Falls! 
 
I’m at @VoyageursView Camping, Tubing, & Kayaking with second-generation owners and siblings, Stephanie & Jason. 🏕️  
 
Voyageur’s View has been helping capture Minnesotans’ adventurous spirit since 1985. https://t.co/8ptxV0hKuH

I’m kicking off a Northwest & Northern Minnesota rural tour.

First stop: Halstad — where I met with Red River Valley Electric Co-Op & local leaders at a new HQ.

They're working on moving overhead powerlines underground & improving reliability with the help of federal funding. https://t.co/5HIJPLZAjk

I'm thinking of Connie Clery, who passed this week at 95.
 
After losing her daughter Jeanne to campus violence in 1986, Connie turned grief into action, and her advocacy led to the Jeanne Clery Act which changed how colleges approach campus safety. Her legacy can be seen in https://t.co/iam9UKNKIA
RT @MarkWarner: Trump – on the precipice of signing one of the most important and fully bipartisan housing laws in years – has suddenly cha…

Eleven years ago, same sex marriage was legalized across the country with the historic Obergefell decision.
 
Today on Equality Day, we celebrate marriage equality and continue to fight for the passage of the Equality Act!

The bipartisan ROAD to Housing Act will help build more homes, cut red tape, lower housing costs, and expand homeownership.
 
It's passed both the House and Senate and the President has the bill to sign.
 
He should sign it now. No delays. https://t.co/YfCOnyqfbS

Congratulations to Minnesota Teacher of the Year David Davis! A music teacher at Park Spanish Immersion Elementary in St. Louis Park, David is an innovator in student-centered curriculum who encourages students to take an active role in their learning. https://t.co/Fj0fY2dBVw

Nothing is more Minnesota than hockey (besides maybe tater tot hotdish).
 
So I’m helping add hockey sticks and our state bird — the loon — to the Smithsonian 250th birthday cake with cake artist Grace Pak. https://t.co/5xptVjAm5a

Because of the Dobbs decision overturning women’s right to reproductive health, too many women now have stories similar to Amanda's.

This cannot stand. https://t.co/lkyW4XgD7v

Four years ago, Roe v. Wade was overturned — stripping away women's reproductive rights across the country, creating a patchwork of different laws, & unleashing chaos.

We must codify into law women's fundamental right to make decisions about their own bodies. https://t.co/EzxDLa7S6F

Does the President have the legal authority to end vote-by-mail?

Let’s find out. https://t.co/444fgA0mmv

I partnered with @ChuckGrassley to introduce the American Innovation and Choice Online Act. Our bipartisan bill will restore online competition and affordability by preventing the world's largest digital platforms from abusing their power. https://t.co/HeRecySLfn

We recently lost my former staffer Asal Sayas after an incredibly brave battle with cancer.
 
I’m joining with @SenMarkey & @RepAndreCarson — with the support of Asal's family — to introduce legislation named in her memory to tackle the growing rate of cancer in adults under 50. https://t.co/3s0gDh4nr7
Great meeting with Minnesota students on the Rural Electric Youth Tour in Washington. They’re visiting DC so they can learn about advocacy and civic engagement up close. https://t.co/SEsHqI4QoH

Dreamers were brought here as children and only know the United States as their home.

They serve in our military, work at our schools, serve as police, own businesses, & care for patients as nurses.

We need to pass the Dream Act and give DACA recipients clear legal status. https://t.co/NbsLCDaMgK

It’s now been one year without my friend Melissa Hortman and her husband Mark.

Melissa was a true public servant who dedicated her life to serving Minnesotans with integrity and compassion. May we all be instruments of peace like Melissa. https://t.co/SEcPiWzNgz

Every year, it’s my honor to nominate some of Minnesota’s talented young people to attend our military service academies.

I know that these students will make our state proud as they continue Minnesotans’ long tradition of answering the call to serve our nation! 🇺🇲 https://t.co/PftdXnvs35
RT @SenateAgDems: Happy National Dairy Month! Grateful for our dairy farmers who work hard every day to deliver healthy and delicious dairy…

RT @SenAlexPadilla: Trump promised his war with Iran would be short-lived, but we just surpassed 100 days with no end in sight.
 
The costs…
Farmers and families are continuing to face rising costs caused by the Administration's across-the-board tariffs and war in Iran. https://t.co/jLh9w8AgIZ
Happy National Seersucker Day! Great to join my colleagues from both sides of the aisle in kicking off summer with this time-honored tradition. https://t.co/H2eiaseEJG

Last year, a horrific act of political violence took former speaker of the Minnesota House Melissa Hortman and her husband Mark Hortman from us.

Melissa was a good friend and she always led with integrity, courage, and a tenacious focus on improving people’s lives. https://t.co/bXB65gPYa0

We’ve secured federal funding to restore the historic Aerial Lift Bridge in Duluth. 
 
As the late Congressman Jim Oberstar would say, this isn't a Democratic or Republican bridge – it’s everyone's bridge. https://t.co/rljr6tRkwi
RT @SenateAgDems: WATCH: Ranking Member Klobuchar’s opening remarks at the oversight hearing on the U.S. Department of Agriculture (USDA) w…

Met with advocates from the Alzheimer's Association to discuss my bipartisan AADAPT Act. Primary care providers are calling for more Alzheimer’s training resources to improve early detection, diagnosis, and care – my bipartisan legislation will deliver tools to support them. https://t.co/wsldEIiVWQ

Great to meet with Coach Phill and Shannon Drobnick in Washington. For 20 years, Phill has helped grow curling in Minnesota and across the country – becoming the most decorated coach in Team USA curling history! 🥌 🇺🇸 https://t.co/LHiiPVxFJT

assessment.posture: No captured Kurdistan social posture yet
assessment.summary: The local archive has not captured a tweet from this account that matches TOR Phi's Kurdistan Lens terms yet. Capture more timeline pages and targeted Kurdistan searches before drawing a social-media conclusion.
assessment.whatItWasLike: No Kurdistan, Kurds, KRG/IKBY, Erbil, Peshmerga, Northern Iraq, PKK/YPG, Iraq/Syria, Yazidi/Sinjar, or energy-linked tweet is present in the profile window yet.
